title: Bladeborn

{{Quote|The Bladeborn are a splinter faction of Sith who believe in the superiority of edged weapons as a means of focusing the dark side. Their tools include traditional lightsabers as well as exotic vibroblades such as the Sith Tremor sword.|Restelly Quist, ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force''|<ref name="JP" />}}
The '''Bladeborn''' were a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] splinter faction that originated as elite [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] of [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]]. They believed edged [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] were better than [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] for focusing the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and, as a result, trained in combat with [[Steel/Legends|steel]] [[Sword/Legends|swords]]. Select members of the Bladeborn were awarded the [[masterblade]] [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] as well as the [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]]-laced [[Sith Tremor Sword]]s.

==Description==
[[File:SithAssassin.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Bladeborn originated as the most elite of Darth Revan's Sith assassins (a Sith assassin pictured).]]
The Bladeborn were an organization descended from the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] tradition of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]].<ref name="JP" /> Its 
[[Human/Legends|Human]] members were [[Sith Blademaster|Sith blademasters]]<ref name="KOTORCG" /> who believed that edged [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]] was superior than [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] for the purpose of focusing the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="JP" /> Consequently, the Bladeborn were dedicated to the mastery of combat with<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> [[Steel/Legends|steel]]<ref name="CSWE tremor sword">{{CSWECite|III|262|tremor sword}}</ref> [[Sword/Legends|swords]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> Only a few members of the group attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[masterblade]], which was given to those individuals who had survived at least ten [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duels]] against a lightsaber-[[Lightsaber combat/Legends|wielding]] opponent.<ref name="CSWE masterblade">{{CSWECite|II|293|masterblade}}</ref>

The Bladeborn wore [[Sith mask/Legends|Sith masks]] and were equipped with [[Stealth field generator/Legends|stealth field generators]] as well as [[Infrared goggles/Legends|infra-goggles]] that allowed them to see in low-light conditions. They wielded [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blasters]]<ref name="KOTORCG" /> and exotic [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]]. The latter included the [[Sith Tremor Sword]],<ref name="JP" /> which was laced with the lightsaber-resistant [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]]<ref name="CSWE tremor sword" /> and was awarded to masterblades as an indication of their skill and rank.<ref name="CSWE masterblade" /> The Bladeborn would also use [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsabers]], albeit even less frequently than Sith Tremor Swords.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

Members of the Bladeborn were highly acrobatic and stealthy individuals who were highly proficient at using [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]] such as swords and lightsabers and also adept at the use of [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]]. They were also Force-sensitive, being able to [[Force power/Legends|draw upon]] [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to<ref name="KOTORCG" /> improve their martial prowess, channel the dark side in the form of a [[Force Enrage|state of rage]], and let them [[Force speed/Legends|move quickly]] and [[Force jump/Legends|jump]] great heights and distances.<ref name="Saga">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''</ref> The Bladeborn spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] and the [[Sith (language)|Sith]] [[Language/Legends|languages]].<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==History==
The Bladeborn originated as the most elite members of the [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]], which were established by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] as part of his [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|empire]] at some point between [[3959 BBY]] and [[3957 BBY]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> By [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="3956 BBY" /> the Bladeborn had become<ref name="KOTOR" /> a Sith splinter faction, and they were still active<ref name="JP" /> by [[990 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="Date">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-1.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 1 of 4)|quote=Okay, so we've established that this particular copy of the [[Book/Legends|book]] dates from [[115 BBY]]. But the content inside is […] dating from the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[990 BBY]].}}</ref> [[Chief Librarian/Legends|Chief Librarian]] [[Restelly Quist/Legends|Restelly Quist]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] mentioned the group in the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi training/Legends|trainee]] [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''.<ref name="JP" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Bladeborn were introduced in the [[2003]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The [[2010]] sourcebook ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', included the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] mini-adventure "[[The Mask of Darth Nihilus]]." The latter included the character [[Collan Eislo]], who was a [[Noble/Legends|noble]] and the leader of the [[Apex Society]] [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]]. Eislo was presented as being well-trained in melee combat and wielding a [[Sith sword]], and the mini-adventure suggested using for him the roleplaying statistics of a Bladeborn Sith [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]].<ref name="TUR">{{WizCite|book=The Unknown Regions|story=The Mask of Darth Nihilus}}</ref>
